input.prompt:
I have a .NET MAUI app with a ContentPage containing labels, buttons, and
a background color. Everything is hardcoded to light colors right now.
I want the app to automatically switch between light and dark mode based
on the device's system theme. Show me how to use AppThemeBinding to make
this page theme-aware in XAML and explain the C# alternative.

I have a .NET MAUI app with a ContentPage containing labels, buttons, and
a background color. Everything is hardcoded to light colors right now.
I want the app to automatically switch between light and dark mode based
on the device's system theme. Show me how to use AppThemeBinding to make

input.initialPrompt:
I have a .NET MAUI app with a ContentPage containing labels, buttons, and
a background color. Everything is hardcoded to light colors right now.
I want the app to automatically switch between light and dark mode based
on the device's system theme. Show me how to use AppThemeBinding to make
this page theme-aware in XAML and explain the C# alternative.

data.content:
## AppThemeBinding in .NET MAUI

**XAML approach** â€” use `AppThemeBinding` to declare light/dark values inline:

```xml
<ContentPage xmlns="http://schemas.microsoft.com/dotnet/2021/maui"
             xmlns:x="http://schemas.microsoft.com/winfx/2009/xaml"
             x:Class="MyApp.MainPage"
             BackgroundColor="{AppThemeBinding Light=#FFFFFF, Dark=#1E1E1E}">

    <VerticalStackLayout Padding="20" Spacing="15">

        <Label Text="Welcome"
               FontSize="28"
               TextColor="{AppThemeBinding Light=#000000, Dark=#E0E0E0}"
               HorizontalOptions="Center" />

        <Label Text="This page adapts to your system theme."
               FontSize="16"
               TextColor="{AppThemeBinding Light=#444444, Dark=#AAAAAA}" />

        <Button Text="Click Me"
                BackgroundColor="{AppThemeBinding Light=#6200EE, Dark=#BB86FC}"
                TextColor="{AppThemeBinding Light=#FFFFFF, Dark=#000000}" />

    </VerticalStackLayout>
</ContentPage>
```

The framework reads `Application.Current.RequestedTheme` and picks `Light` or `Dark` automatically. When the OS theme changes at runtime, bound values update live â€” no restart needed.

---

**C# alternative** â€” use `SetAppTheme<T>()` extension method:

```csharp
public class MainPage : ContentPage
{
    public MainPage()
    {
        BackgroundColor = Colors.White;
        this.SetAppTheme<Color>(
            BackgroundColorProperty, Colors.White, Color.FromArgb("#1E1E1E"));

        var title = new Label { Text = "Welcome", FontSize = 28 };
        title.SetAppTheme<Color>(
            Label.TextColorProperty, Colors.Black, Color.FromArgb("#E0E0E0"));

        var button = new Button { Text = "Click Me" };
        button.SetAppTheme<Color>(
            Button.BackgroundColorProperty,
            Color.FromArgb("#6200EE"),
            Color.FromArgb("#BB86FC"));

        Content = new VerticalStackLayout
        {
            Padding = 20, Spacing = 15,
            Children = { title, button }
        };
    }
}
```

`SetAppTheme<T>(property, lightValue, darkValue)` is the code-behind equivalent of `{AppThemeBinding Light=â€¦, Dark=â€¦}`.

---

**Key points:**

| Topic | Detail |
|---|---|
| **Trigger** | Follows `OSAppTheme` from the device; updates automatically |
| **Scope** | Works on any `BindableProperty` (colors, images, strings, etc.) |
| **Resources** | For reuse, define themed colors in `ResourceDictionary` and reference with `{StaticResource}` or combine with `AppThemeBinding` |
| **Manual override** | Set `Application.Current.UserAppTheme = AppTheme.Dark` to force a theme |
data.reasoningText: The user is asking for a tutorial/explanation on how to use `AppThemeBinding` in .NET MAUI to support light/dark mode theming. This is a knowledge question â€” they want a code example and explanation, not changes to an existing repo. I'll provide a concise but complete answer.
